phase1.explanation:
Arrays store multiple values of the SAME type:

Declaration: type[] name = {values};
or: type[] name = new type[size];

Access: name[index] — index starts at 0!
Length: name.length (no parentheses!)

Common mistake: ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException — accessing an index >= length

For loop vs For-each:
• For loop: gives you index control, can modify elements
• For-each: cleaner syntax, but read-only (can't modify array)

⚠️ Arrays are fixed size! You can't add or remove elements after creation.

Pro tip: Use for-each for loops where you just need to read values.

phase3.prompt:
Write a program that:
1. Creates an array of 5 integers: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25
2. Prints each element using a for loop
3. Prints each element using a for-each loop
phase3.openingDialogue: Time to store multiple values in one variable! 📚
